text: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好,这里是BBC英语教学的“六分钟英语”节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Bekka. Do you enjoy cooking, Neil?
zh: 我是尼尔。我是贝卡。尼尔,你喜欢做饭吗?

text: Yes, I do enjoy cooking. I think it's quite relaxing, especially after a busy day.
zh: 是的,我很喜欢做饭。我觉得做饭挺放松的,尤其是在忙碌了一天之后。

text: How about you, Bekka? I enjoy cooking when I'm feeding other people.
zh: 你呢,贝卡?我喜欢在给别人做饭的时候做饭。

text: But usually after a busy day at work, I don't enjoy cooking for myself. Well, we're talking about cooking today.
zh: 但通常忙碌工作一天后,我不太愿意为自己做饭。好了,今天我们要聊的话题就是做饭。

text: And most of us can at least boil an egg, but not everyone can make it as a professional chef in a world -famous restaurant.
zh: 我们大多数人至少会煮个鸡蛋,但不是每个人都能在世界知名餐厅里当上专业大厨。

text: Being a top chef requires passion, dedication and hard work.
zh: 成为顶级大厨需要热情、专注和努力。

text: Many chefs work 15 hours a day, six days a week.
zh: 许多厨师每天工作15个小时,每周工作六天。

text: And busy kitchens cooking expensive food are very stressful places to work.
zh: 而忙碌的厨房里制作昂贵的菜肴,工作压力也非常大。

text: So why do they do it? In this episode,
zh: 那他们为什么还要这样做呢?在这一期节目中,

text: we'll hear from one South African chef who started her own award -winning restaurant in the country known as the home of fine dining, France.
zh: 我们将听到一位南非厨师的故事,她在被誉为精致餐饮之乡的法国,开创了自己屡获殊荣的餐厅。

text: As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 像往常一样,我们会学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all this episode's vocabulary,
zh: 别忘了,你可以在我们的网站上找到本期的所有词汇,

text: along with the transcript, on our website bbclearningenglish .com. But first, I have a question for you now.
zh: 还有文字稿,网址是bbclearningenglish.com。不过首先,我先问你一个问题。

text: The top prize for any professional chef are Michelin stars – awards given to restaurants offering outstanding cooking.
zh: 对任何专业厨师来说,最高荣誉就是米其林星——授予提供卓越美食的餐厅的奖项。

text: But who is the winner of the most Michelin stars ever?
zh: 但谁是历史上获得米其林星最多的厨师呢?

text: Is it... a Italian chef Enrico Bartolini
zh: 是……a. 意大利厨师恩里科·巴尔托利尼

text: b French chef Joël Robuchon or c
zh: b. 法国厨师乔尔·卢布松,还是c.

text: British chef Gordon Ramsay? I'm going to say a
zh: 英国厨师戈登·拉姆齐?我猜是a,

text: the Italian chef Enrico Bartolini because I went to Italy recently and the food was amazing.
zh: 意大利厨师恩里科·巴尔托利尼,因为我最近去了意大利,那里的美食太棒了。

text: OK, we'll find out the answer later in this episode.
zh: 好的,我们会在节目后半段揭晓答案。

text: Born in South Africa, chef Chantelle D 'Artagnan
zh: 出生于南非的厨师香黛尔·达尔塔尼昂,

text: started her culinary career at a three Michelin star restaurant in the exclusive Mayfair area of London.
zh: 在伦敦高级的梅费尔区一家米其林三星餐厅开启了她的厨艺生涯。

text: Here she tells Ruth Alexander, presenter of BBC World Service programme The Food Chain, how it all got started.
zh: 在这里,她向BBC世界服务频道节目《食物链》的主持人露丝·亚历山大讲述了这一切是如何开始的。

text: What happened is I had graduated from the Prudyth College of Food and Wine
zh: 当时的情况是,我从普鲁迪斯食品与葡萄酒学院毕业后,

text: and decided to send my CV to all the three Michelin star restaurants that I could find at that moment in the Michelin Guide.
zh: 决定把简历寄给当时《米其林指南》里所有能找到的三星餐厅。

text: So you aimed high from the off? I aimed high.
zh: 所以你一开始就目标高远?是的,我目标很高。

text: The only thing that they could say was no and what would I lose?
zh: 他们最多也就是说不,我又能损失什么呢?

text: I received a call from chef Paul Rhodes to say when can you start?
zh: 我接到了大厨保罗·罗兹的电话,他问我什么时候可以开始上班。

text: Wow. And this sort of just catapulted my career.
zh: 哇。这一下子就把我的事业推上了快车道。

text: Chantelle sent her CV to the world's top restaurants. CV is short for curriculum vitae,
zh: 香黛尔把简历投给了世界顶级餐厅。CV是curriculum vitae的缩写,

text: a document listing someone's education, qualifications and work experience which they write when looking for a job.
zh: 是一种记录个人教育背景、资历和工作经历的文件,人们在求职时会准备这样一份材料。

text: In American English, it's called a resume. Chantal applied to only Michelin -starred winning restaurants.
zh: 在美式英语中,它被称为resume。香黛尔只申请了获得过米其林星的餐厅。

text: She aimed high, meaning she was ambitious. Chantal says the worst they could do is say no,
zh: 她目标高远,意味着她很有雄心。香黛尔说,最坏的结果不过是被拒绝,

text: a phrase which means you shouldn't be afraid of trying to do something because the worst outcome is being rejected.
zh: 这句话的意思是不要害怕去尝试,因为最坏的结果也不过是被拒绝而已。

text: Luckily for Chantal, the answer was yes,
zh: 幸运的是,对香黛尔来说,答案是肯定的,

text: and learning her trade in a top London restaurant catapulted, meaning accelerated, her career.
zh: 在伦敦一家顶级餐厅学艺,让她的事业“突飞猛进”,也就是加速发展。

text: At the age of 26, Chantal left London and returned home to open her own restaurant, Mosaic, near Pretoria in South Africa.
zh: 26岁时,香黛尔离开伦敦,回到家乡,在南非比勒陀利亚附近开设了自己的餐厅——Mosaic。

text: She'd been inspired by botanical cuisine – the use of edible flowers and herbs to decorate and flavour food.
zh: 她的灵感来自植物美食——即用可食用的花卉和香草来装饰和调味食物。

text: Here, Chantal tells more to BBC World Service programme The Food Chain.
zh: 在这里,香黛尔向BBC世界服务频道节目《食物链》进一步讲述了她的故事。

text: When I opened Mosaic in South Africa, I knew
zh: 当我在南非开设Mosaic餐厅时,我知道

text: I wanted something entirely different and something which diners had not seen.
zh: 我想要一种完全不同的东西,一种食客从未见过的东西。

text: I wanted to have my own signature and to stand out. And I think especially
zh: 我想拥有自己的标志性风格,与众不同。我觉得尤其是

text: for botanical cuisine in South Africa, it was very much unknown. Chantal developed her own signature.
zh: 在南非,植物美食当时几乎无人知晓。香黛尔形成了自己的招牌风格。

text: A signature item is something that is typical of or strongly associated with a particular person or place.
zh: 招牌事物,是指某种典型地、强烈地与某个人或某个地方联系在一起的东西。

text: For example, whiskey is Scotland's signature drink.
zh: 比如,威士忌就是苏格兰的招牌饮品。

text: Chantal's signature is cooking botanical food.
zh: 香黛尔的招牌就是植物美食。

text: Chantal's Mosaic restaurant is currently at the Château de Teniers in northern France,
zh: 香黛尔的Mosaic餐厅目前坐落在法国北部的泰尼耶城堡,

text: where it's listed among the top 200 restaurants in the world.
zh: 并被列入世界前200强餐厅之一。

text: And in 2017 she won the title Best Female Chef in the World.
zh: 2017年,她还获得了“世界最佳女性厨师”的称号。

text: These achievements have helped Chantal stand out, to be more noticeable and better than other chefs.
zh: 这些成就帮助香黛尔脱颖而出,变得比其他厨师更引人注目、更出色。

text: Chantal is still waiting, but surely it's only a matter of time until she wins those coveted Michelin stars.
zh: 香黛尔还在等待,但她赢得那些梦寐以求的米其林星,想必只是时间问题了。

text: Speaking of which, what was the answer to your question, Bekka?
zh: 说到这个,贝卡,你之前那个问题的答案是什么?

text: Ah yes, I asked you earlier who is the winner of the most Michelin stars ever?
zh: 啊,对,我之前问你,谁是历史上获得米其林星最多的厨师?

text: I said that it was the Italian chef, Enrico Bartolini.
zh: 我说是意大利厨师恩里科·巴尔托利尼。

text: But unfortunately it was incorrect. Oh no! The right answer is B,
zh: 但很遗憾,答案不对。哦不!正确答案是B,

text: Joël Robuchon, our French chef with 31 stars, the most decorated chef in history. OK,
zh: 法国厨师乔尔·卢布松,他拥有31颗星,是历史上获得荣誉最多的厨师。好的,

text: let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with curriculum vitae, CV or resume in American English,
zh: 让我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是curriculum vitae,也就是CV,美式英语中叫resume,

text: a document outlining someone's education, qualifications and work experience, which they prepare when looking for a job.
zh: 是一种概述个人教育背景、资历和工作经历的文件,人们在求职时会准备。

text: To aim high means to be ambitious and set high goals.
zh: to aim high 意思是雄心勃勃,设定远大的目标。

text: The phrase, the worst they can do is say no, means don't be afraid of trying to do something for the fear of rejection.
zh: the worst they can do is say no 这句话的意思是,不要因为害怕被拒绝而不敢去尝试。

text: If something is catapulted, it's suddenly accelerated or launched forward.
zh: 如果某事被catapulted,意味着它被突然加速或推动向前发展。

text: A signature item is something that a person or place is known for.
zh: 招牌事物(signature item)是指某人或某地以此闻名的东西。

text: And finally, to stand out means to be more noticeable or better than other similar things. Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 最后,to stand out 意思是比其他同类事物更显眼、更出色。我们的六分钟又到时间了。

text: If you enjoyed this episode, you'll find a quiz and worksheet to practise the vocabulary we've learnt on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 如果你喜欢这一期节目,可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到练习本期词汇的小测验和练习表。

text: See you again soon, soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们下次再见,再见,现在,就先说再见啦。
